verdict_prose: **Verdict — the architecture has no effective security boundary at any layer.** Every cross-cutting security pattern that would normally catch a control failure is absent or deliberately disabled.
framing: Three cross-cutting weaknesses drive the majority of findings:

weaknesses.description: The RSA private key used to sign session tokens is committed to the public repository; every authentication control is undermined from the moment the repo is cloned. Rotation requires a code change and a redeploy, and anyone with read access to the source already holds the current value.

The authentication and catalog-search flows both assemble SQL by string-concatenating untrusted request fields. There is no ORM-mediated parameter binding on these paths, so the same template-literal pattern that exfiltrates the password column also bypasses the credential check.

Multiple Angular components bypass the framework's default DOM sanitization to render user-controlled HTML directly. Without a Content Security Policy as defense in depth, every reflected or stored string is a viable XSS sink.
